page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.s3.medium is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine instance within the ecs.s3 server family. It is configured with 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores, resulting in a 1.0 thread-per-core ratio without hyperthreading. The instance is allocated 4.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 1.0 GB of RAM per core. It operates on a KVM hypervisor.

This server type does not include any local NVMe or SSD storage, requiring external block storage volumes for operating system and data hosting. It features zero GPUs and does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

With its dedicated CPU allocation and low memory-to-CPU ratio, the ecs.s3.medium offers a compute-focused profile. It is suitable for workloads that require dedicated processing power but have minimal memory footprints, such as lightweight web servers, microservices, batch processing tasks, and basic computational workloads.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.s3.medium is an x86_64 virtual machine instance within the ecs.s3 family, running on a KVM hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores with a single thread per core, providing dedicated compute resources without hyperthreading. The instance is equipped with 4.0 GB of system memory, yielding a 1.0 GB RAM-to-core ratio. It does not include local storage or GPU hardware, meaning all storage must be provisioned externally. Given its dedicated CPU allocation and low memory footprint, this instance is designed for compute-focused workloads with low memory requirements, such as lightweight web serving, microservices, and basic batch processing.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.s3.medium is a KVM-based x86_64 instance featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs, 4.0 GB of RAM, and no local storage, designed for compute-focused workloads with low memory requirements.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.s3.medium is a KVM-based x86_64 instance with 4 dedicated vCPUs, 4.0 GB RAM, and no local storage for compute-focused workloads.
tagline: A dedicated x86_64 compute instance featuring four dedicated vCPUs and four gigabytes of memory for low-memory processing workloads.
bullet_points: 4.0 GB of system memory (1.0 GB per core)
KVM hypervisor with single thread per physical core
No local storage or bundled GPU resources
Suited for lightweight web serving and microservices
